results.content:
What started as a straightforward Cogent IP Transit order quickly turned into one of the most frustrating vendor experiences of my career. I respect what Cogent Communications (AS174) has built. Theyre one of the largest Tier 1 networks with global reach. But nothing has tested my patience more than our attempt to order service from them over the last month. Alliance Broadband is preparing to launch a new IP core network as part of our rural broadband expansion in Florida. This multi-year effort includes circuits from several providers. Weve handled contracts and technical requirements before, but nothing as painful as Cogent. Our discussions with their sales team began in July. Things started strong: good communication, aggressive pricing, and executed paperwork by mid-August. Then [...] MinRTT, Incero, PutFile

Alex, I'm literally in the EXACT same boat with Cogent Communications, waiting over a month for a 100gig LOA because of their "abuse" team, I actually signed up in June 2025. The irony for my situation is that I've used cogent for two of my past businesses without any abuse or billing issues over the past two decades. There is no way to see what the issue is, communicate with anybody about it, etc. Somebody broke their signup process and it's costing them money.

Ehson Ali [...] should not have justified the endless scrutiny and slow responses that followed. As the weeks dragged on, our go-live date was put at risk. Calls went unanswered. Emails delayed. Escalations to directors and VPs produced no progress, even when we offered to meet with the abuse team directly. In the end, despite submitting what felt like everything short of our social security numbers, we still couldnt get approval. At that point, we stopped trying. I share this not to vent, but to highlight a lesson. As a small company, we depend on partners who move quickly, communicate clearly, and provide value upfront. results.title: Cogent Communications - Networking - Spiceworks Community
results.content:
#### Overall Rating

5 out of 5 stars

The worst service i have ever seen!  
Firstly Cogent could not provide reliable datacenter internet connection for one of our customers. So he had to migrate to another provider to do not loose his clients.  
But now after we sign the Cogent collocation contract ourselves, I was told about inability to provide the 24/7 helping hands service despite it was announced as a part of the contract. Now our account managers say that it was their mistake and there is no such service in this certain datacenter provided. I have just received a message that despite of our complains they could terminate the contract, but we must pay penalties for whole year.  
I WOULD STRONGLY ADVISE ANYBODY TO AVOID USING COGENT SERVICES.

#### Overall Rating

1 out of 5 stars [...] #### Overall Rating

5 out of 5 stars

The initial setup was drawn out and poorly handled, and then took more than 2 weeks to deliver the speeds that we contracted for. After that, we only had 1 service call for slow speeds that was resolved in about 2 days.

Since then, and until recently, service via Cogent has been nearly flawless. However, now that Cogent has segregated their retail and wholesale customers into separate groups and dropped a severe QoS hit to the wholesalers, the speeds are nowhere near what we pay for, nor is the reliability anywhere near serviceable. With multiple hits to our VoIP and VPN services, and more than 24 hours (going into a weekend) without any sort of response to our issues… Cogent no longer suits the needs of our business.

#### Overall Rating [...] #### Overall Rating

1 out of 5 stars

I can speak very highly about Cogent. I have used Cogent as our ISP for the last 3 years. The circuit has been reliable 99.9% of the time. I have a 100Mbps connection at the office. The speed at the router is always close to 100Mbps (97 to 99Mbs). Cogent’s tech support is 1st class, always responsive, and will work through the issue until it resolved to the client’s satisfaction. I recommends them over the more well know ISP providers.

#### Overall Rating

5 out of 5 stars

Always seems rock solid when we use their services (temporary high speed links in Kansas City area). Always get the promised bandwidth at good prices.

#### Overall Rating

5 out of 5 stars

results.title: Cogent (CCOI) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
results.content:
Shares of internet service provider Cogent Communications (NASDAQ:CCOI) fell 14% in the afternoon session after it reported weak second-quarter results that missed Wall Street's expectations for both revenue and earnings. The company's revenue fell 5.5% year-over-year to $246.2 million, while its loss per share of $1.21 was nearly 30% worse than analysts had anticipated. The disappointing results highlighted several underlying issues for investors. Cogent's cash burn worsened, with the company using $100.2 million in cash during the quarter, a significant increase from the same period last year. Furthermore, the report pointed to a concerning balance sheet, with over $2.3 billion in debt compared to just $306.7 million in cash, raising questions about the company's financial stability.
